level_signals: Accountable to results reported at the executive level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree or higher, ideally in a technical, finance, or quantitative field.
5+ years of product management experience, with meaningful time in payments, commercial/B2B fintech, or financial infrastructure.
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful time in payments, commercial/B2B fintech, or financial infrastructure

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deep understanding of how money moves - wires, ACH, real-time rails, stablecoins - and what's different about commercial payments: higher stakes, multi-party authorization, beneficiary management, batch operations.
Experience shipping APIs that external developers integrate against. You think in contracts, error codes, idempotency keys, and developer experience.

Ability to cut through swirl and define the requirements. You can take ambiguous client needs and translate them into clear, testable requirements that an engineering team can execute without constant clarification.
Proven cross-functional partnership skills and ability to work in matrixed environment
Experience working in regulated environments - you know how to partner with compliance, legal, and risk teams while keeping the product experience usable.
Technical credibility - you can read an API spec, review a sequence diagram, and discuss system design with engineers. You self-service data.
Strong communication, forward lean and AI-fluency

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience at a bank, neobank, or banking-as-a-service provider - particularly on the commercial or treasury side.
Familiarity with real-time payment rails (FedNow, RTP) and/or stablecoin settlement infrastructure.
Experience with developer-facing products at companies like Stripe, Plaid, Marqeta, Unit, Treasury Prime, Modern Treasury, or similar.

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska

scope.surface: Payments product experience and API/developer platform for Big Business Banking (BBB) - SoFi's zero-to-one commercial banking platform for institutional clients
scope.business_model: B2B commercial banking / financial infrastructure (national bank charter backed)
primary_metrics_inferred: API integration success rate (time from signed contract to first successful API payment)
Go-to-market readiness and rail launch adoption
core_responsibilities: Define payment product strategy for Big Business Banking - sequence the roadmap across various payments capabilities
Own the client-facing payment experience - design end-to-end UX for how treasury teams initiate, approve, track, and reconcile payments across portal and API
Own the API product and developer experience - define payment and webhook API contracts, error handling, idempotency, and versioning
Write PRDs and requirements for Bank Platform - serve as the 'customer voice' to the Bank Platform team
Define payment operations and exception handling
Partner with Risk and Compliance on payment-level controls
Own go-to-market for new payment capabilities

green_flags: Zero-to-one commercial banking platform - high ownership and impact opportunity
Clear articulation of responsibilities and required technical depth
Specific payment rails named (FedNow, RTP, ACH, SEN, wires) - signals genuine domain seriousness
AI-fluency listed as a requirement rather than just a nice-to-have, suggesting modern product culture
EEO and San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance mentioned - inclusive hiring signals
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed despite mention that 'base pay range for this role is listed below' - appears to be missing from the JD as scraped
Location type is unclear - no city or remote policy explicitly stated in the body (Hawaii/Alaska exclusion is the only geographic signal)
Equity not mentioned despite senior-level role at a public fintech
